key_insight: Preference Pareto Exploration (PPE) lets users navigate deep multi-task trade-offs interactively by moving along the Pareto manifold in the direction they prefer, rather than relying on repeated weighted-sum retraining.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a practical way to steer expensive deep multi-task models toward desired accuracy trade-offs while reducing the need for explicit Hessian computations.
method: Predictor step travels tangentially along the Pareto-optimal manifold according to the decision maker’s preference.
Corrector step projects to a new nearby trade-off that reflects the chosen preference.
Krylov subspace techniques estimate tangent-space information using only matrix-vector products via automatic differentiation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that PPE was demonstrated on toy problems and deep learning examples, showing functionality and performance across both settings.
watch_item: Interactive Pareto navigation still depends on reliable preference specification and an efficient implementation of matrix-vector products for large deep models.
paper_title: Interactive Pareto navigation for deep multi-task learning
